question_text: A stretched rubber band is released and flies across the room. Explain which energy stores are involved in this process.
correct_answer: The stretched rubber band has elastic potential energy stored in it. When released, this elastic potential energy is transferred to kinetic energy as the rubber band moves across the room.
explanation: This tests understanding of energy transfer between two specific stores. Students must identify elastic potential energy in the stretched object and recognise how it converts to kinetic energy during motion.
